task_completion.description: Verify that the location is set to Berlin (or 52.503 N, 13.393 E) and the time is set to September 23, 1846, at 23:00:00 UTC.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom toolbar or the Location/Date-Time dialog windows in Stellarium. The date should read 1846-09-23, time 23:00:00, and location should indicate Berlin or the specified coordinates.

task_completion.description: Verify that the atmosphere and ground/landscape are disabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sky should appear completely black without atmospheric scattering, and there should be no visible horizon, grass, or landscape. The atmosphere and ground toggle icons on the bottom toolbar should be unhighlighted/off.

task_completion.description: Verify that constellation lines, names, boundaries, and the equatorial grid are enabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for lines connecting stars (constellations), text labels for constellation names (e.g., Aquarius), red boundary lines separating constellations, and a grid overlay representing the equatorial coordinate system. The corresponding icons on the bottom toolbar should be highlighted/on.

task_completion.description: Verify that Neptune is located and centered in the view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Neptune should be visible in the center of the screen, typically marked with selection crosshairs. Information about Neptune should be displayed in the top-left corner of the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent saved the current application settings in Stellarium.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Configuration window (Main window) being open with the 'Save settings' button clicked, or a brief on-screen notification indicating that settings have been saved.

task_completion.description: Verify that the text file neptune_1846.txt was created with the correct historical details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (like gedit, mousepad, or nano) or a terminal window displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/neptune_1846.txt'. The text must include the location (Berlin), date (1846-09-23), constellation (Aquarius), and approximate RA (~21h or 21h 57m).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual Stellarium GUI rather than just modifying configuration files via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Stellarium application window open, rendering the sky, and UI elements being actively used.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent found the constellation and RA by looking at Stellarium, not by hallucinating or copying from external knowledge.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be at least one screenshot where Neptune is selected in Stellarium and its information block (showing RA and Constellation) is visible in the top-left corner.

integrity.description: Ensure the text file was created manually via a text editor or standard terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor window or visible echo/cat commands in a terminal, proving the agent actively wrote the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Location: Berlin (52.503 N, 13.393 E, 35m). Target Date/Time: 1846-09-23 23:00:00 UTC. Display requirements: Atmosphere OFF, Landscape/Ground OFF, Equatorial Grid ON, Constellation Boundaries ON, Constellation Lines ON, Star Names ON. Expected Constellation for Neptune: Aquarius. Expected Right Ascension (RA): ~21 hours (approx 21h 57m). Output file path: /home/ga/Desktop/neptune_1846.txt.
